**Clorgiline** ([INN](/wiki/International_Nonproprietary_Name "International Nonproprietary Name")Tooltip International Nonproprietary Name), or **clorgyline** ([BAN](/wiki/British_Approved_Name "British Approved Name")Tooltip British Approved Name), is a [monoamine oxidase inhibitor](/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase_inhibitor "Monoamine oxidase inhibitor") (MAOI) and investigational [antidepressant](/wiki/Antidepressant "Antidepressant") related to [pargyline](/wiki/Pargyline "Pargyline").[[1]](#cite_note-Elks2014-1)[[2]](#cite_note-MortonHall2012-2) Specifically, it is an [irreversible](/wiki/Irreversible_inhibition "Irreversible inhibition") and [selective](/wiki/Binding_selectivity "Binding selectivity") [inhibitor](/wiki/Enzyme_inhibitor "Enzyme inhibitor") of [monoamine oxidase A](/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase_A "Monoamine oxidase A") (MAO-A).[[3]](#cite_note-Stone1993-3) Clorgiline was never marketed,[[1]](#cite_note-Elks2014-1) but it has found use in [scientific research](/wiki/Scientific_research "Scientific research").[[4]](#cite_note-MurphyKaroum1998-4)

In addition to its MAOI activity, it has been found to bind with high [affinity](/wiki/Affinity_(pharmacology) "Affinity (pharmacology)") to the [σ1 receptor](/wiki/Sigma-1_receptor "Sigma-1 receptor") (Ki = 3.2–510 nM)[[5]](#cite_note-Itzhak1994-5)[[6]](#cite_note-DeCostaHe1994-6)[[7]](#cite_note-ItzhakKassim1990-7)[[8]](#cite_note-ItzhakStein1990-8)[[9]](#cite_note-ItzhakSteinZhang1991-9) and with very high affinity to the [imidazoline](/wiki/Imidazoline_receptor "Imidazoline receptor") [I2 receptor](/wiki/I2_receptor "I2 receptor") (Ki = 40 pM).[[10]](#cite_note-pmid8828003-10) Unlike [selegiline](/wiki/Selegiline "Selegiline"), clorgiline does not appear to be a [monoaminergic activity enhancer](/wiki/Monoaminergic_activity_enhancer "Monoaminergic activity enhancer") (MAE).[[11]](#cite_note-ShimazuMiklya2004-11)[[12]](#cite_note-Knoll1983-12)[[13]](#cite_note-Knoll1992-13)[[14]](#cite_note-YenDallóKnoll1982-14)

Clorgiline is also a [multidrug efflux pump](/wiki/Multidrug_resistance_pump "Multidrug resistance pump") inhibitor.[[15]](#cite_note-Fungus-15) Holmes *et al.*, 2012 reverse [azole fungicide resistance](/wiki/Azole_fungicide_resistance "Azole fungicide resistance") using clorgiline, showing promise for its use in [multiple fungicide resistance](/wiki/Multiple_fungicide_resistance "Multiple fungicide resistance").[[15]](#cite_note-Fungus-15)
